channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Dog waste in grassed children area, 24th April
item.description:
Large dog who comes from Lingfield house ( ground floor ) constantly use children’s park! irresponsible owner never picks up after him , I watch him from my back window let’s it do it business and then children come from local school and have to dodge the mess it’s disgusting, it does stipulate No Dogs but obviously can’t read ,
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Lancaster Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:

item.title: Dumped Rubbish, 24th April
item.description:
There is a significant amount of rubbish that has been dumped on the embankment alongside Tetty Way. The photos don’t show everything but it’s a lot!

item.title: Large amount of fly tipping, 24th April
item.description:
Large amount of fly tipping next on public footpath next to 1 Yalding Grove & in front metal gates for the alleyway.

item.title: Loose NEW paving slab outside No 16, 24th April

item.title: Litter on Charlton Church Lane, 24th April
item.description:
I saw a council lady with a dustpan and brush get into her van and drive off. With litter all along the road.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Charlton Church Lane, Charlton
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 71 Cambridge Road NW6 5AG, 24th April
item.description:
As you can see from the current state (and as shown in the attached photo), rubbish is consistently overflowing from the bins and spilling out onto the pavement and street. Waste is scattered everywhere, including food containers, packaging, and household items. This is not only unpleasant but also poses a clear hygiene and health risk, potentially attracting pests and creating an unsafe environment for residents and passersby. We cannot open the windows of our rooms because flies have infested the area and the situation keeps getting worse day by day!

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cambridge Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Street cleaning - Street bin needs emptying

item.title: Contractors damaging new paving, 24th April
item.description:
Highway contractors are breaking up the new pavement with heavy machinery to access pipes
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Pembury Place, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree Problem, 24th April
item.description:
Good morning We own a house on Wyncham Avenue DA15 8EU, we are on the side that backs onto the stream, number 31 for your information. There are 3 sycamore trees that overhang our garden, the pigeons roost/sit on the branches and drop their poop into the garden/patio area, it has got to the stage where it is dangerous for the persons that live here, which include 2 young children, all occupants already live with health conditions. Pigeon droppings can pose health risks, primarily through inhalation of dried droppings, which may carry fungal spores and bacteria harmful to humans, especially those with weakened immune systems. Health Risks Pigeon droppings can harbor pathogens that cause diseases such as histoplasmosis, cryptococcosis, psittacosis, and Salmonella infections. The main danger arises when dried droppings crumble and release microscopic fungal spores and bacteria into the air, which can be inhaled and settle deep in the lungs. Direct contact with droppings can also transmit bacteria like Salmonella if contaminated hands touch food or the mouth. Histoplasmosis: A respiratory disease caused by the fungus Histoplasma capsulatum, often found in soil enriched with bird droppings. Infection occurs through inhalation of spores and can be severe in immunocompromised individuals. Cryptococcosis: Caused by Cryptococcus neoformans, this fungal infection is more dangerous for people with weakened immune systems, potentially leading to systemic illness. Psittacosis: A rare bacterial infection from Chlamydia psittaci, causing flu-like symptoms, fever, and sometimes pneumonia. Transmission occurs via inhaling dust from dried droppings. Salmonella and E. coli: Bacteria present in pigeon feces can cause gastrointestinal illness if ingested through contaminated surfaces. We would really appreciate it if these trees could be cut down as soon as possible, they are really quite big now and the problem is only going to get worse. Kind regards Mr and Mrs Phillips
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 27, Wyncham Avenue, Longlands, Sidcup, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Glengall Road , opp no 70, 24th April
item.description:

item.title: Shattered glass over the pavement, 24th April
item.description:
There is a lot of shattered glass, covering the whole pavement area.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 76, Sloane Street, Chelsea, Greater London

item.title: Litter picking from our front lawn, 24th April
item.description:
On daily basis your team should get rid of rubbish from outside communal areas(lawns) & pick up litter, remove fly-tipped rubbish around the estate. We don’t see any cleaner doing that on daily bases. After the windy days lots of detritus and random rubbish ends up on our front lawn.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glengall Road, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pipe blocked from large pond into small pond, 24th April
item.description:
The pipe from the middle pond into the small pond has very little water flowing through it, as it is blocked.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): River Park View, Goddington, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Penrose Grove is being used as a dumping ground., 24th April
item.description:
Penrose Grove has started to be a fly-tipping area. It also rarely swept or litter picked up and is becoming dirty and unhealthy.please could you fix this.Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Carter Place, Walworth, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
